issues.body: `live_leases = _ledger_leases(ledger_root)` runs once before iterating, and the code itself notes that allocator claims land remotely rather than updating this checkout. Consequently, after the snapshot is read, a worker or another dispatcher can acquire the same crate and still pass this check. There is also a deterministic same-loop case: with an initially empty snapshot, an earlier `review:` item can claim a crate remotely, then a later `fix:` item for the same crate sees the same empty snapshot; because `allocator.claim` only compares same-prefix leases, both claims can succeed. Calling the predicate immediately before each claim does not make its underlying data live. The wiring test only rewrites the local file between separate `run_items` calls, so it cannot expose snapshot staleness or mixed-lane claims within one call.
issues.fix_hint: Enforce repository/package cross-lane exclusion inside the allocator's SHA-bound CAS transaction using the remote ledger state used for the claim. Add a non-vacuous test with review and fix (or worker and review) contenders for one crate where the second claim observes/rejects the first claim committed after the initial plan/checkout snapshot. If a preflight remains as defense in depth, do not describe it as closing the claim-time race.
issues.title: Stale per-tick snapshot does not enforce cross-lane exclusion at claim time

verdict.summary: The added predicate is locally fail-closed and its unit cases are meaningful, but the wiring does not provide the claimed immediately-before-claim cross-lane exclusion. `_dispatch_review_items` reads one checkout snapshot before the loop and reuses it for every review/fix item, while claims are committed to the remote ledger. A lease created after that read—including by an earlier item in the same loop—remains invisible, and the allocator's same-prefix predicate cannot serialize a review claim against a fix/worker claim. This leaves the exact cross-lane co-run the change is intended to prevent, so the change is not sound or complete.
